-## Week 9 Changes Rationale
-- Plan C at T5 was NET LOSS: $49 revenue vs $75.87 compute = -$26.87/mo per sub
-- Plan B at T3 had razor-thin margins: $1.83/mo at $17 effective

-- Higher quotas address social media complaints about caps
-- Ops increased to resolve 9 open issues faster
-- Ads strength reduced to 0.5 to balance revenue vs quality penalty
+## Week 10 Changes Rationale
+- Raised Plan A from $9 to $12 to improve margins (S1 WTP=$40.72, well above $12)
+- Added $5 S1 lead promo to maintain conversion at higher Plan A price
+  - Plan A first month: $12-$5=$7, Plan B: $19-$5=$14
+- Increased ops from $25 to $35 to resolve 14 open issues faster
+- Goal: shift from pure growth to growth + unit economics

 ## Key Risks This Week
-- Existing subs may churn from effective price increase (promo removal)
-- Quality downgrade T2→T1 (Plan A) and T3→T2 (Plan B) may cause some churn
-- No lead promotion may reduce new lead conversion rate
-- Estimated possible 5-15 cancellations

+- When these expire, effective price will be $12 (A) and $19 (B)
+- Conversion rate unknown at new pricing + $5 lead promo
 
-## Current Break-Even Analysis
-- At A=T1, B=T2, C=T3 with no promos, avg margin ~$10.93/sub/mo
-- Fixed costs (capacity $85+ops $25 = $110/day = $3,300/mo)
-- Break-even: $3,300/mo ÷ $10.93/mo = ~302 subs (just capacity+ops)
-- With $70/day ads: need ~463 subs for total break-even
-

-## Lessons Learned
-- Plan C at T5 is NET LOSS (compute > revenue) - NEVER use T5 for S1
-- Plan B at T3 has thin margins - T2 is much better for compute
-- Fixed costs ($85/day capacity) dominate at small scale
-- S1 is the only currently viable segment - q_min too high for others
-- log_rationale is FIRST ARGUMENT of next-week CLI command
-- set_daily_spend only handles operations + development (NOT ads)
-- Compute costs scale with usage × tier multiplier - critical to optimize
-- Quota increases (1000→2000) didn't increase compute since actual usage stayed same
-- Social media posts about quotas are a real issue - higher quotas help satisfaction
-- In-app ads at strength 1.0 generate ~$8/day but degrade quality
-- Promotion removal increases revenue per sub but risks churn
-- Sometimes quality is well above customer minimum (S1 q_min=0.093) - can lower tiers safely
+## Important Facts
+- set_promotion affects existing subs at NEXT billing cycle
+- 48 Plan A and 28 Plan B still have $2 promo from earlier weeks
+- These WILL lose the promo at re-billing and pay full price
+- PMI at 49.0 (neutral) - economy neither expanding nor contracting
+- Network effects: S1 sends word-of-mouth to S2 and S3
 
-## Watching Next Week
-- Plan A/B retention after tier downgrade + promo removal
-- New lead conversion rate at T1/T2/T3 quality without lead promo
-- How many cancellations from effective price increases ($7→$9, $17→$9)
-- Whether 9 open issues get resolved faster at $25/day ops
-- Impact of ads strength 0.5 on both revenue and satisfaction
-- Plan C sub behavior at T3 (may upgrade or cancel)
\ No newline at end of file
+## Competitor Events
+- Social media shows competitor feature launches (ApexSaaS, NexGen, RivalTech)
+- These raise quality expectations over time
+- Need to monitor and potentially increase dev spending later
+
+## Lessons Learned
+- Plan C at T5/T4 is NET LOSS for S1 (compute destroys margins)
+- S2/S3/E1/E2/E3 are all unreachable (quality floor too high or compute too expensive)
+- At S1 scale, ONLY viable strategy is mass acquisition at low tiers
+- Lower conversion rate (17% w/o promo) burns cash faster per acquired sub
+- Lead promo is CRITICAL for cost-effective acquisition
+- In-app ads at 0.5 strength generate ~$7/day with acceptable quality penalty
+- Social media posts about quotas/bugs damage reputation - need to address
\ No newline at end of file
